# Resources {.unnumbered}

<br>

A short, opinionated list of further reading and tools.

## Books

  - *The Psychology of Money*, by [Morgan Housel](https://www.morganhousel.com/) [@housel2020psychology]
  - *I Will Teach You to Be Rich* (2nd ed.), by [Ramit Sethi](https://www.iwillteachyoutoberich.com/) [@sethi2019will]
  - *The Algebra of Wealth*, by [Scott Galloway](https://www.profgalloway.com/) [@galloway2024algebra]
  - *The Little Book of Common Sense Investing*, by John C. Bogle [@bogle2017little]
  - *The Simple Path to Wealth*, by J. L. Collins [@collins2016simple]

## Podcasts & channels

  - [Rational Reminder](https://rationalreminder.ca/): Ben Felix and Cameron Passmore's evidence-based investing podcast (also on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@rationalreminder))
  - [Common Sense Investing](https://www.youtube.com/@BenFelixCSI): Ben Felix's short-form video series at PWL Capital
  - [The Prof G Pod](https://www.profgalloway.com/podcasts/) and *Prof G Markets*: Scott Galloway's podcasts on business, markets, and personal finance

## Blogs & sites

  - [morganhousel.com](https://www.morganhousel.com/): Morgan Housel's writing
  - [Collaborative Fund blog](https://collabfund.com/blog/): where many *Psychology of Money* essays first appeared
  - [iwillteachyoutoberich.com](https://www.iwillteachyoutoberich.com/): Ramit Sethi's site, including the [Conscious Spending Plan](https://www.iwillteachyoutoberich.com/conscious-spending-basics/)
  - [PWL Capital research](https://www.pwlcapital.com/category/research/): Ben Felix's firm publishes white papers on housing, factor investing, and human capital
  - [profgalloway.com](https://www.profgalloway.com/): Scott Galloway's weekly *No Mercy / No Malice* newsletter and writing

## Communities

  - [r/personalfinance](https://www.reddit.com/r/personalfinance/): the
    wiki is unusually good
  - [Bogleheads](https://www.bogleheads.org/): forum and wiki around
    low-cost index investing, the community John Bogle inspired

## Tools

  - Spreadsheets (Google Sheets / Excel): still the most flexible option
  - Account aggregators (Monarch, Copilot, YNAB)

## References

::: {#refs}
:::

## Color Palette

The mermaid diagrams throughout this book use a consistent **Atomic Age / Mid-Century Modern** palette. Each color is assigned to a semantic role:

| Role | Color Name | Hex | Text | Usage |
|------|-----------|-----|------|-------|
| green | Forest Green | `#48a56a` | `#F5F2E8` | Root / start / end nodes |
| primary | Ocean Teal | `#0e9aa7` | `#F5F2E8` | Process and action nodes |
| decision | Blush Pink | `#f0cfcf` | `#1C1C1E` | Decision / branching nodes |
| outcome | Mint | `#86ddcd` | `#1C1C1E` | Positive result nodes |
| warn | Atomic Red | `#f44242` | `#F5F2E8` | Warning / negative nodes |
| — | Space Black | `#1C1C1E` | — | Borders, arrows, body text |
| — | Cream | `#F5F2E8` | — | Light text on dark fills |
